Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (24) - Inheritance
Last time I teased that inheritance was coming up next, so naturally this post is all about inheritance (yeah, obviously).
So what is inheritance? Does JavaScript have it? Yep, JavaScript does.

In short, here's the idea, framed with people. Imagine there's a dad, and he has a son (obviously), and the son inherits some of the dad's biological traits. For example, if the dad's eye color is blue, then the eye color the son inherits is also blue.
So let me write a quick JavaScript inheritance version first, just as a reference:

Last time I teased that inheritance was coming up next, so of course this post is all about inheritance. We start with a quick JavaScript refresher (both the prototype version and the ES6 class version), then jump into how Python does it — spoiler: there's no extends keyword, and super works a lot like the one you already know.
